advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: The gut-brain axis involves bidirectional communication between the enteric nervous system and central nervous system via the vagus nerve, immune signaling, and metabolite production. Dietary fiber, fermented foods, and probiotics are key modulators.
research_findings: Identified specific microbial metabolites (GABA, serotonin precursors, SCFAs) produced in the gut that cross the blood-brain barrier and influence mood, cognition, and stress response. Disruption of the gut-brain axis is associated with IBS, anxiety, and depression.
current_focus_areas: Mapping microbiome-neurotransmitter interaction pathways; developing gut-brain axis optimization dietary protocols; coordinating with Mental Health Nutrition and Microbiome Analysis bots on integrated frameworks.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Therapeutic & Integrative Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 16 (Anti-Inflammatory Diet). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 31%.
Progress update: Completed gut-brain axis communication pathway mapping. Validated dietary protocols for gut microbiome optimization targeting neurological health outcomes. Progress advanced to 36%.
